name: A large-scale dataset of pre- and postsurgical MRI data from patients with chronic trigeminal neuralgia (OpenNeuro ds005713)

evidence.quote: we present a large dataset of neuroimaging data from patients with chronic trigeminal neuralgia (112 patients and 48 healthy subjects)

we provide a comprehensive dataset comprising high-resolution neuroimaging data collected from 112 patients with primary trigeminal neuralgia (TN) alongside 48 age- and sex-matched healthy control subjects
A large-scale dataset of pre- and postsurgical MRI data from patients with chronic trigeminal neuralgia

n_subjects.value: 112 patients with trigeminal neuralgia and 48 healthy subjects (160 participants total); 58 patients also have 6-month postsurgical follow-up

evidence.quote: we present a large dataset of neuroimaging data from patients with chronic trigeminal neuralgia (112 patients and 48 healthy subjects)
The study included a total of 160 participants after all the exclusion criteria were applied.
Follow-up data six months after treatment were available for 58 patients.
This prospective study was carried out at our hospital from October 2021 to October 2024
High-resolution 3D T1-weighted gradient echo imaging with the following acquisition parameters
High-resolution 3D T2-weighted turbo spin echo imaging with the following acquisition parameters
Diffusion tensor imaging (DTI) with the following acquisition parameters
Resting-state functional MRI (rs-fMRI) was conducted with the following parameters

Clinical and demographic information for each participant, along with primary MRI findings, neurovascular compression grade (according to the Sindou classification, if suitable), and surgical procedure type are provided in the “participants.tsv” file.

license.needs: OpenNeuro dataset page (ds005713) explicit data-license terms; the only license found in evidence (CC BY-NC-ND 4.0, src e2) is the Crossref content-domain/text-mining license for the journal article, not confirmed as the dataset's own license

provider.needs: Author affiliations or dataset landing page naming the institution that acquired the MRI data / generated the dataset. Evidence only names an unspecified 'our hospital' and separately states that ethics approval came from 'the local ethics committee of the Federal Center of Neurosurgery' — the latter is the ethics-approving body, not confirmed by the text as the data-generating or hosting institution.

region.needs: Dataset/landing page stating the study site's country or city (evidence only names the MRI scanner manufacturer's country, The Netherlands, and the hospital name, not the study location)

evidence.quote: The dataset is freely available on the open access OpenNeuro platform in BIDS format and includes all the required components

access_notes.value: The dataset is freely available on the open access OpenNeuro platform in BIDS format and includes all the required components

evidence.quote: The dataset is freely available on the open access OpenNeuro platform in BIDS format and includes all the required components
The dataset is freely available on the open access OpenNeuro platform in BIDS format and includes all the required components

short_desc_en.value: An open, BIDS-formatted dataset on OpenNeuro (ds005713) with structural (T1w, T2w), diffusion tensor, and resting-state fMRI data plus clinical and demographic information from 112 patients with chronic trigeminal neuralgia (58 with 6-month postsurgical follow-up) and 48 healthy controls.

evidence.quote: The dataset is freely available on the open access OpenNeuro platform in BIDS format and includes all the required components
we present a large dataset of neuroimaging data from patients with chronic trigeminal neuralgia (112 patients and 48 healthy subjects)
Follow-up data six months after treatment were available for 58 patients.

In addition to the MRI data, the dataset consists of clinical and demographic information about the patients, including the type, intensity, and side of pain; duration of the disease; and data on pharmacological treatment.
High-resolution 3D T1-weighted gradient echo imaging with the following acquisition parameters
High-resolution 3D T2-weighted turbo spin echo imaging with the following acquisition parameters
Diffusion tensor imaging (DTI) with the following acquisition parameters
Resting-state functional MRI (rs-fMRI) was conducted with the following parameters

short_desc_es.value: Un conjunto de datos abierto en formato BIDS alojado en OpenNeuro (ds005713) con resonancia magnetica estructural (T1w, T2w), tensor de difusion y fMRI en reposo, ademas de informacion clinica y demografica, de 112 pacientes con neuralgia del trigemino cronica (58 con seguimiento postquirurgico a 6 meses) y 48 controles sanos.

evidence.quote: The dataset is freely available on the open access OpenNeuro platform in BIDS format and includes all the required components
we present a large dataset of neuroimaging data from patients with chronic trigeminal neuralgia (112 patients and 48 healthy subjects)
Follow-up data six months after treatment were available for 58 patients.

In addition to the MRI data, the dataset consists of clinical and demographic information about the patients, including the type, intensity, and side of pain; duration of the disease; and data on pharmacological treatment.

access_steps.step_en: Go to the OpenNeuro dataset page (accession ds005713, referenced by DOI 10.18112/openneuro.ds005713.v1.0.1)
access_steps.step_es: Ve a la pagina del dataset en OpenNeuro (accession ds005713, referenciado por el DOI 10.18112/openneuro.ds005713.v1.0.1)

access_steps.step_en: Download the dataset directly, no account or credentialing required (open access platform)
access_steps.step_es: Descarga el dataset directamente, sin cuenta ni credencial (plataforma de acceso abierto)

access_steps.step_en: Explore the BIDS structure per participant (anat, func, dwi folders) and read participants.tsv / participants.json / README.txt for clinical and acquisition details
access_steps.step_es: Explora la estructura BIDS por participante (carpetas anat, func, dwi) y lee participants.tsv / participants.json / README.txt para detalles clinicos y de adquisicion

starter_code.python:
import pandas as pd
import nibabel as nib

# participants.tsv holds clinical/demographic data, MRI findings,
# Sindou neurovascular compression grade, and surgical procedure type
participants = pd.read_csv('participants.tsv', sep='\t')
print(participants.head())

# Each subject folder has anat/, func/, dwi/ sub-folders (BIDS layout)
t1w = nib.load('sub-001/anat/sub-001_T1w.nii.gz')
print(t1w.shape)
starter_code.r:
library(data.table)

# participants.tsv holds clinical/demographic data, MRI findings,
# Sindou neurovascular compression grade, and surgical procedure type
participants <- fread('participants.tsv')
head(participants)
